breakdown: Root: to break, divide; vibhaṅga “division, analysis”.

vibhaṅgasuttaṁ.meaning: the Vibhaṅga Sutta, “the Analysis” (a discourse title)

vibhāvayanti.meaning: they clearly understand; they make clear
vibhāvayanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present, causative (of vibhāvayati, vi + √bhū)
breakdown: Causative stem of √bhū: to make become, make clear.

vibhāvayanti clearly.meaning: they clearly understand (duplicate listing of vibhāvayanti)
vibhāvayanti clearly.note: uncertain: the key includes the stray English word “clearly” from the source layout; the Pāli lemma is vibhāvayanti.

vibhūsanaṭṭhānā.meaning: from occasions of embellishment; (abstaining) from adornment and decoration
breakdown: Place, occasion (with sandhi doubling of ṭ).

vicariṁ.gram: verb, aorist 1st person singular of vicarati

vicikicchitaṁ doubt vijjati.meaning: doubt is found; doubt exists (run-together listing of vicikicchitaṁ and vijjati)
vicikicchitaṁ doubt vijjati.note: uncertain: this key runs together the lemma vicikicchitaṁ “doubt”, its English gloss, and vijjati “is found” from the source layout.

viditadhammo.meaning: one who has known the Dhamma; having found the teaching

viditaṁ.gram: past participle of vidati (√vid), accusative/neuter singular

vigatakathaṅkatho.meaning: with doubt gone; free from perplexity

vigatamalamaccherena.meaning: with (a mind) free from the stain of stinginess

vigatamiddho.meaning: free from drowsiness; one who is awake

vigatapaccayo.meaning: disappearance condition (a conditional relation in the Paṭṭhāna)

vigatābhijjho.meaning: with covetousness gone; free from greed
breakdown: Covetousness, greed (the initial a merges with the preceding a: vigatā-).

vigatāsava.meaning: free from the taints; one whose mental defilements are gone — a saint

vigatūpakkilesā.meaning: with the defilements completely gone; free from the minor defilements
breakdown: Completely gone (vi + gata; the final a merges with the following u: vigatū-).

vigayha.meaning: having plunged into; having entered, experienced

viggayha.meaning: having quarrelled, having disputed; having taken hold of
breakdown: Prefix: apart (with sandhi doubling of g).
Root: to seize; viggaṇhāti “to quarrel, dispute”.

vighātapakkhiko.meaning: siding with vexation; leading to distress and opposition
breakdown: Siding with, belonging to the party of, leading to.

vighātapariḷāhā.meaning: distress and fever; annoyance and burning grief

vihangama.meaning: moving through the air, flying; a bird

vihaññati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular, passive of vihanati (vi + √han)
breakdown: Root: to strike; passive hañña- “to be afflicted”.

viheṭhakajātiko.meaning: of a harassing nature; aggressive by character
breakdown: Having the nature of, of the character of (from jāti).

viheṭhayanto.gram: present participle of viheṭhayati, masculine nominative singular

vihiṁsasaññī.meaning: perceiving (with thoughts of) cruelty; aware of injuring

vihāramidhamāhu.meaning: this they have called the dwelling here

breakdown: Root: to leave, abandon (reduplicated present stem jaha-).

vijite.meaning: in the realm; in the conquered land, in the country
vijite.gram: neuter noun (past participle of vijināti), locative singular
breakdown: Root: to conquer; vijita “what has been conquered — the realm”.

vijjati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular, passive of vindati (√vid)
breakdown: Root: to find; passive vijja- “to be found, exist”.

vijjācaraṇasampanno.meaning: endowed with knowledge and conduct (an epithet of the Buddha)
vijjācaraṇasampanno.summary: One of the nine standard epithets of the Buddha in the Buddhānussati formula.
breakdown: Endowed with, possessed of (saṁ + panna, past participle).
vijjācaraṇasampanno.note: uncertain: the lexicon row carries a stray gloss from an adjacent entry (“carriage maker + clan”); the meaning given here follows the morphological breakdown vijjā + caraṇa + sampanna.

vikiraṇiṁ.gram: feminine agent noun, accusative singular (of vikiraṇī)

breakdown: Prefix: apart, away (with sandhi doubling of k).
Root: to prop, obstruct; vikkhambheti “to suppress, discard”.

vikālabhojanā.meaning: from eating at the wrong time; (abstaining) from meals at improper hours
breakdown: Time (vikāla: the wrong time, after midday).

vikālavisikhācariyānuyogo.meaning: the habit of roaming the streets at unseemly hours

breakdown: Root: to smile, be amazed; vimhāpana “causing astonishment, deception”.

vimutti.goenka: Vimutti is liberation — the release of the mind from craving and defilement which is the goal of the path. Full deliverance is realized through one's own practice and experience, culminating in vimuttiñāṇadassana, the knowledge and vision of liberation.

vimuttiñāṇadassanasāraṁ.meaning: having the knowledge and vision of liberation as its essence

vimutto.gram: past participle of vimuccati, masculine nominative singular

vinayadharānaṁ.meaning: of those who bear the Vinaya; of the upholders of the discipline

vinayadharānaṁ holding.meaning: of those who bear the Vinaya (duplicate listing of vinayadharānaṁ)
vinayadharānaṁ holding.note: uncertain: the key includes the stray English word “holding” from the source layout; the Pāli lemma is vinayadharānaṁ.

vinayapurekkhāratā.meaning: deference to the Vinaya; putting the discipline first
breakdown: The state of placing before, deference to (from purekkhāra).
